{{Infobox character
| name = Judge Suriel
| aliases = Suriel’s Presence, Suriel
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|sacred art to reverse time, [[Presence]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|, can anchor a world to order through her connection to the Way}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|, can pass through the Way}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|, can look into Fate}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|, can tear open a rift into the Way}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[The Phoenix|the Phoenix]], Sixth Judge of the Abidan Court, [[judge|Judge]], [[celestial messenger]], [[Phoenix]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|, [[Hound]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|, leader of the Abidan}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Abidan Court]], [[Abidan]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|, [[The Eight-Man Empire|Eight-Man Empire]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|healer, [[judge|Judge]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|, caretaker}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Seat of the Accused]]}}
| appears_in = Cradle
| introduced = [[Wintersteel (novel)|Wintersteel]]
}}

''See also: [[Judge Suriel Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel, also known as The Phoenix{{!}}the Phoenix, is the Sixth [[Judge]] of the [[Abidan Court]]. Recognized as the greatest healer in existence, she holds a high-ranking position within her organization and serves as a vital bridge for cooperation between the other [[Judges]]. Her role involves maintaining order, stability, and protection across various worlds by utilizing her connection to Way{{!}}the Way. While she possesses immense power capable of restoring entire continents, reversing time, and bringing the dead back to life, she is also bound by strict regulations regarding interference in mortal affairs. Physically, Suriel appears as a perfected version of her original self, characterized by seamless white armor, bright green hair, and purple eyes containing complex formations that allow her to perceive [[Fate]]. She is accompanied by a [[Presence]]—a spectral, communicative [[construct]] that assists with data processing and provides psychological companionship. Her primary tools are her Mantle, a flowing river of white flame, and Suriel's Razor{{!}}Suriel’s Razor, an incredibly powerful instrument that functions as both a weapon and a precision tool for healing. Throughout her tenure, she has navigated complex political tensions within the [[Abidan Court]], including conflicts with other [[Judges]] like [[Makiel]] and [[Gadrael]], while also providing guidance and visions to significant individuals such as Lindon{{!}}Wei Shi Lindon.}}

== Unsouled ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">21</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a healer and The Phoenix{{!}}the Phoenix.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6">''Unsouled'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses transparent gray ghostlines running from the back of her head down to her fingers.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel wears a seamless white uniform made of inch-thick liquid armor.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's liquid armor could be mistaken for [[Forged madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses a meter-long shaft of blue [[steel]] weapon that she can banish to [[Sanctum]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's eyes contain a ring of symbols that function as tools to help her see the flow of [[fate]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can observe target locations from a thousand kilometers away using her [[Presence]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a white-armored woman with green hair and ghostly lines leading from her fingertips to her skull.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a celestial messenger.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel uses a sacred art to reverse time.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses green hair and a pale face.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has purple eyes.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's organization refers to the world as '[[Cradle]]'.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel gives [[Lindon]] a token that she can sense across worlds and beyond time.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel's token]] is tied to [[Lindon]] with strings of [[fate]] and cannot be broken or lost.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel promised to provide a token to facilitate finding the individual later.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel provided [[Lindon]] with visions.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16">''Unsouled'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a [[Phoenix]] and the Sixth [[Judge]] of the [[Abidan Court]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20">''Unsouled'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel's Presence]] retrieves and transmits reports as words, images, and impressions.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's designation is zero-zero-six.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has the power of healing.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Soulsmith ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a character who spoke to [[Lindon]] about his timeline.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2">''Soulsmith'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel suggested how far [[Lindon]] could travel down his [[Path]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel challenged [[Lindon]] to reach great heights.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Blackflame ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] has seen Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel with his own eyes.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2">''Blackflame'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is associated with the The Eight-Man Empire{{!}}Eight-Man Empire.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel mentioned the [[Sword Sage]] to [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c7">''Blackflame'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel showed [[Lindon]] a [[dragon]] beneath the sea.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is an [[Abidan]] who can anchor a world to order through her connection to Way{{!}}the Way.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13">''Blackflame'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is known as The Phoenix{{!}}the Phoenix.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a 'lodger' associated with [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c15">''Blackflame'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel witnessed the death of an [[Iteration]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21">''Blackflame'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel was familiar with the [[Arelius family]] and their ancestors.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Skysworn ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel told [[Lindon]] he had thirty years.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c1">''Skysworn'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a member of the [[Court]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2">''Skysworn'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has demonstrated absolute Authority{{!}}authority.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is an [[Abidan]] who stands over the sixth division and has purple eyes and emerald hair.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Underlord ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's armor is seamless and white.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1">''Underlord'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses Suriel's Razor{{!}}Suriel’s Razor.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's armor cracked during the battle.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] owes Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel his life.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel showed [[Lindon]] the [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Uncrowned ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel reported a missing world to [[Makiel]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c2">''Uncrowned'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Wintersteel ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] had seen the room in Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's vision years before.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel may have sent [[Kiuran]] to remove [[Lindon]] from contention.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's visit was a significant event for [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel once told [[Lindon]] that all [[Paths]] can be reduced to one: improve yourself.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel gave [[Lindon]] a second chance.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel had a vision of the future showing something gigantic tramping [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Judge Suriel]] is a favorite of someone mentioned by the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Bloodline ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">23</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can tap into her mantle to revert a world towards a state of order.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1">''Bloodline'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can pass through Way{{!}}the Way to enter an [[Iteration]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel's Presence]] can issue commands to the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel showed [[Lindon]] an ending.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel had a vision of the future.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel descended from heaven to bring [[Lindon]] back from death.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel saw an attack that was supposed to happen in thirty years.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] has memories of Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is someone who has died.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel banished [[Li Markuth]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has the power to turn back time or rewrite truth.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16">''Bloodline'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses a marble that [[Lindon]] rolls between his fingers.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel commanded a transmission to begin.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel's Presence]] spoke to her.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's hair is bright and vivid emerald.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's space is a projection of treasures she has collected.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is restricted by rules regarding the degree of interference she can provide.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble burns with a blue flame.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has scripted eyes.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has armored fingertips.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel gave [[Lindon]] a marble to facilitate finding him after or during his ascension.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses the [[Mantle of Suriel]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel spends power to keep the world intact and maintain the connection between the [[Iteration]] and Way{{!}}the Way.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Reaper ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">14</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can connect to the network of [[Abidan]] all over existence.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has ghostly gray correlation lines running from the back of her skull to her knuckles.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozmanthus Arelius]] had no compatibility with The Phoenix{{!}}the Phoenix.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble contains light.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a new, young woman Ozriel had known for centuries.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has a [[Presence]] that lives inside her mind.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15">''Reaper'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses armor that flows around her and runes that spin in her eyes.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can look into [[Fate]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel uses her [[Razor]] to remove toxic energy and parasites.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel glimpsed the trade that would be made for [[Makiel]]'s action.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel intervened when the conflict between Ozriel and [[Makiel]] destabilized surrounding [[Iterations]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] fulfilled everything Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel promised him.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has bright green hair and eyes that are bright purple rings of Script{{!}}script.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27">''Reaper'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel vanished in a rising tide of blue light.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Dreadgod ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">10</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses [[Presence]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1">''Dreadgod'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel transported herself to the [[Seat of the Accused]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel stated that something was made to detect [[Judges]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a [[Judge]] who has the job of repairing worlds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a woman with bright green hair and a shining [[sword]] split into many branches.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c20">''Dreadgod'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a caretaker for Ozriel.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is tasked with keeping Ozriel under observation and preventing him from using powers without permission.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has the ability to reconstitute entire continents from nothing.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can tear open a rift into Way{{!}}the Way.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is capable of performing the work of an entire squad of lesser Phoenixes.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Waybound ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">19</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a character who floats in the emptiness over [[Vesper]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c1">''Waybound'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a character who uses her [[Presence]] to warn of barriers and obstacles in Way{{!}}the Way.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble has a restorative [[aura]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c2">''Waybound'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is the Sixth [[Judge]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel's power manifests as blue, fiery wings.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has runic eyes that can scan the future.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a [[Hound]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has the ability to revive the dead.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is someone who might bring a miracle.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22">''Waybound'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a character Ozriel mentions to [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a healer who can fight in the Void and possesses a Mantle.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has blue, healing fire.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel had a vision of the [[Wandering Titan]] wading through mountains.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a [[Judge]] who rarely sits in judgment of mortals or ascended ones.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel might come back for [[Li Markuth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel gave [[Lindon]] Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36">''Waybound'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel can find things anywhere in any world.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel wears a white uniform.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c37">''Waybound'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel possesses a glass marble containing a single blue candle-flame.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c37" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel has an opening in the [[Sixth Division]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is considered too compassionate for her own good.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is an entity that can restore lost eyes.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]] has Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel on his side.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a figure who may keep the [[Reaper]] alive.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Gadrael]] threatened to hand [[Cirian]] over to Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is a leader of the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Judge Suriel{{!}}Suriel is one of the [[Judges]] and looked exasperated.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|As Suriel had once told him, “There are a million Paths in this world, Lindon, but any sage will tell you they can all be reduced to one. Improve yourself.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|"And tell Judge Suriel’s favorite I hope to see him too."<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|"Suriel descending from heaven to bring him back from death would have been the only miracle he ever received."<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“That was made to detect Judges,” Suriel said.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“Tell Suriel I’m counting on her,” Ozriel sent.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
